item.title: Coupang Warns of 2026 Slowdown After Data Breach Hits Spending
item.description: Coupang Inc. warned revenue growth will slow this year after a bigger-than-expected March-quarter loss, reflecting the extent to which a historic cyber-intrusion is depressing spending across South Korea’s biggest online retail platform.

item.title: US Unveiled Plan to Reopen Hormuz Hours Before Trump Shelved It
item.description: Top US officials spent Monday and Tuesday offering new details on the role the US military will play in helping ships pass through the Strait of Hormuz as part of President Donald Trump’s bid to break Iran’s chokehold over the waterway.

item.title: Sun Pharma Explores Funding Mix for $12 Billion Organon Deal
item.description: Global lenders to Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. are weighing multiple financing options for its proposed $12 billion acquisition of New York-listed healthcare company Organon & Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Infratil Shares Surge as CDC Signs Monster Data Center Deal
item.description: CDC Data Centres is forecasting a surge in earnings over the next three years after agreeing Australia’s largest data center contract, stoking the value of its largest holder Infratil Ltd.

item.title: Samsung Hits $1 Trillion Valuation, Joining TSMC in Elite Club
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. has reached a $1 trillion market valuation after booming demand for chips used in artificial intelligence saw the world’s largest memory maker’s stock more than quadruple over the past year.

item.title: Indonesia Tightens Rule on FX Purchases After Rupiah Hit New Record Low
item.description: Indonesia tightened rules on dollar purchases to defend the rupiah after it weakened to an all-time low.

item.title: Sydney Hedge Fund Regal Sees Inflows Jump for Inflation Hedges
item.description: Sydney-based fund manager Regal Partners Ltd. is on track to hit A$2 billion ($1.4 billion) in net inflows this year, as conflict in the Middle East spurs demand for investment strategies offering a hedge against inflation.

item.title: Private Credit Fund Touts Quick Profit Buying Debt at 65 Cents
item.description: A New Mountain Capital private credit fund that sold almost half-a-billion-dollars of assets at a discount earlier this year and used some of the cash to scoop up beaten-down loans says the strategy is already paying off.

item.title: South Korea’s Inflation Accelerates on Higher Oil Price Impact
item.description: South Korea’s consumer inflation picked up as surging energy costs began to feed more forcefully into the domestic economy, with price gains matching the fastest pace since July 2024.

item.title: Trump to Meet With Brazil’s Lula Thursday for Economic Talks
item.description: President Donald Trump will host Brazilian counterpart Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva Thursday, amid tensions over the Iran war and its impact on the global economy.

item.title: Australian Toll Operator Atlas Arteria Rejects Biggest Shareholder’s $5.3 Billion Takeover Bid
item.description: Sydney-listed toll road operator Atlas Arteria Ltd. rejected a takeover bid from its biggest shareholder IFM Investors Pty, saying the bid was too low.

item.title: New Zealand Unemployment Unexpectedly Falls as War-Hit Looms
item.description: New Zealand’s jobless rate unexpectedly declined in the first quarter as fewer people sought work, with firms bracing for a squeeze on earnings from the Middle East oil-price shock filtering through the economy.

item.title: Gold Steady as Fragile Ceasefire Holds in Gulf After Flareup
item.description: Gold was steady after a gain as the US-Iran ceasefire continued to hold despite reports of a cargo vessel being targeted, easing fears of an escalation that would fuel inflation risks.

item.title: New Zealand Finance Firms See 30% Deposit Surge Outpacing Banks
item.description: New Zealand finance companies are attracting a surge of new money as the nation’s new deposit insurance levels the playing field with banks, according to the Reserve Bank.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Track US Gains on Truce Optimism: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia are set to follow Wall Street higher, while oil steadied after its biggest drop in two weeks, as signs that a ceasefire in the Middle East is holding eased fears of a broader conflict that could threaten the global economy.

item.title: Oil Holds Decline as US Says Offensive Phase of Iran War Is Over
item.description: Oil held a decline as the US said offensive operations against Iran are over as it shifts to protecting shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, which remains largely closed.

item.title: Oaktree BDC Marks Down Software Loans, Flags 26% AI Exposure
item.description: Oaktree Capital Management cut the value of one of its private credit funds by almost 4% as the firm marked down its software assets.

item.title: Argentina Upgraded by Fitch on Milei’s Economic Overhaul
item.description: Argentina’s credit score was upgraded by Fitch Ratings, signaling growing confidence in President Javier Milei’s push to overhaul the economy and secure financing to cover the country’s upcoming debt obligations.

item.title: Compass Shares Surge After 2Q Revenue Forecast Beats Estimates
item.description: Compass Inc. shares soared as much as 21% in after-hours trading on Tuesday after the real estate brokerage company forecast stronger-than-expected quarterly revenue.

item.title: Prudential Profit Rises Even as Firm Grapples With Japan Pause
item.description: Prudential Financial Inc.’s first-quarter profit beat Wall Street’s expectations, relieving pressure on Chief Executive Officer Andy Sullivan as he contends with a sales pause in one of its biggest markets.

item.title: Sixth Street BDC Cuts Its Dividend After Posting Quarterly Loss
item.description: A private credit fund managed by Sixth Street Partners lowered its dividend and reported a quarterly loss, citing credit spread widening and declining valuations.

item.title: End Quarterly Earnings Reports? Here’s the Debate
item.description: For more than 50 years, public companies in the US have been required to report earnings on a quarterly basis. That would change if a proposal by regulators allowing twice-yearly financial disclosures is adopted.

item.title: Latam Airlines Changes Guidance on Fuel Cost Despite Profit Jump
item.description: LATAM Airlines Group SA posted quarterly earnings that exceeded expectations, but updated their full-year guidance to reflect higher jet fuel prices as Latin America’s largest carrier by market value starts to see the impact of rising costs.

item.title: Russian Fuel Tanker Stalls Off Cuban Coast Amid US Blockade
item.description: An oil tanker transporting Russian fuel to Cuba appears to have stalled roughly 1,000 miles (1,600 kilometers) off the coast, dealing a fresh blow as the island faces its worst fuel crisis in decades.

item.title: Beijing’s Veto of Meta’s Manus Deal Signals a Shift in the Global AI Race
item.description: Beijing’s veto of the Meta-Manus deal has shaken the AI industry. What this means for Chinese startups aiming for the global stage.

item.title: Traders Ramp Up Bets Warsh’s Fed Could Hike Rates Before Cutting
item.description: Bond traders are boosting wagers that the Federal Reserve’s next policy move could be an interest rate hike rather than a cut.

item.title: Charlotte Airport Gets Approval For $215 Million in Financing
item.description: Charlotte received the green light on Tuesday to issue $215 million in revenue bonds that will finance a new runway at Charlotte Douglas International Airport.

item.title: AMD Gives Upbeat Forecast After AI Data Center Demand Surges
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc., the second-biggest maker of computer processors, gave a strong forecast for the current period, showing that it’s cashing in on the flood of AI spending.

item.title: Super Micro Gives Profit Outlook Signaling Costs Under Control
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc. gave a forecast for profit in the current quarter that topped analysts’ estimates, signaling the company is controlling the costs of getting its powerful AI servers into customers’ hands.

item.title: Tinder Returns to New User Registration Growth in Sign of Turnaround
item.description: Match Group Inc. reported first-quarter revenue that beat analysts’ estimates as a decline in Tinder users moderated, suggesting its turnaround strategy is resonating with younger daters.
